why: Asked for 7-year survival; countered at 5 with trade-secret carve-out surviving indefinitely — our standard ceiling.
Asked for open-ended survival; rejected — indefinite survival of general confidentiality is a red line, only trade secrets survive indefinitely.
Asked 3-year survival matching term; accepted — at or below our 5-year ceiling.
Asked 10 years; countered at 5 — we never exceed 5 for general information.
Conceded 5 years (from our opening 3) in exchange for them accepting destruction certification.
Countered 6-year ask at 5; explained ceiling is firm policy tied to records retention.
2-year survival accepted; short survival fine when residuals excluded.
Countered indefinite ask at 5 + trade-secret carve-out; they accepted.
Rejected survival measured from each disclosure (rolling); must run from termination — measurable and auditable.
Countered 8-year ask at 5. Ceiling held in all ten matters now.
Refused to drop non-solicit where the counterparty meets our key engineers in diligence.
Reduced non-solicit 18->12 months against a broader definition of general solicitation carve-out.

detail: Three asks in Halloway round 2. Term-survival (evidenced, maturity 10/10): counter the 8-year request down to our firm 5-year ceiling with the indefinite trade-secret carve-out (red line: never exceed 5 years).
